title: Apply Now: Junior Collaborator – Institutional Engagement and Development Actors (Remote, IIHL)
summary: Are you passionate about international law, humanitarian protection and advancing sustainable development? The Apply for Junior Collaborator Institutional Engagement Development Actors (IIHL) represents a unique opportunity to join a world-renowned organisation shaping global policy and partnerships in refugee law and migration law. Position Title Junior Collaborator Institutional Engagement Development Actors Department of International Refugee Law and Migration Law International Institute of Humanitarian Law (IIHL), Sanremo, Italy (Remote) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Application Deadline Applications Close on 5th March 2026 (Rome CET time) Work Location Remote / Home-based (with reliable internet) Travel to Sanremo (Italy), Geneva (Switzerland) or other duty stations may be required and fully covered by IIHL. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Contract Details Contract Type: 6-month collaboration contract Remuneration: ~ 500 per month Scholarships: Complimentary access to Department s online learning courses Hours: Full-time remote role with possible attendance at meetings in Rome CET time zone ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Role Overview Under guidance from the Head of the Institutional Engagement Unit, the Junior Collaborator will: Conduct research on development actors and humanitarian development peace nexus issues Map partnership and funding opportunities with global development institutions Support online and in-person capacity-sharing and learning initiatives Track partnerships and contribute to key strategic work of the Department This role is ideal for early-career professionals seeking first-hand experience with international organisations and a gateway to future roles in humanitarian law and development sectors. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Key Responsibilities 1. Mapping Research Research development actors including governmental, UN, regional, and financial institutions Identify partnership and funding possibilities for capacity building and strategic projects. Analyze trends in protection, displacement, and sustainable responses. 2. Support Outreach Support capacity-sharing initiatives like online courses. Maintain and update the Department s internal partnerships tracker. Assist with documentation and reporting on development engagement activities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Qualifications Skills Required Mandatory 1. University degree in Social Sciences, International Relations, Political Science, Economics, Development Studies, or related field. 2. Strong organizational, written, and oral communication skills. 3. Fluency in English (other UN languages are a plus). 4. Commitment to humanitarian and protection work in displacement contexts. Desirable 1. Experience with international development projects or institutions. 2. Familiarity with refugee, migration, and statelessness law frameworks. 3. Knowledge of instructional design or online capacity-building platforms. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Why This Opportunity Matters This role is strategically positioned at the intersection of humanitarian action, development, and global partnerships. By focusing on development actors , you ll engage with institutions shaping responses to displacement, resilience, and global cooperation all while enhancing your professional credentials in a globally respected institution. Benefits of the Position Professional experience with an international legal humanitarian organisation Exposure to global development and refugee protection coordination Career development through learning scholarships Remote flexibility with optional travel fully funded by IIHL Priority consideration for future roles within the Department ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); How to Apply Submit the following through the official IIHL Careers Portal: Required Documents: 1. Updated CV 2. Motivation Letter Apply here Tip: The Institute may start interviews before the deadline if strong candidates are identified early. The International Institute of Humanitarian Law embraces diversity and inclusion all qualified applicants are encouraged to apply regardless of gender, nationality, ethnicity, religion, disability, age, sexual orientation, or background. Applications from individuals with forced displacement and statelessness experiences are especially welcome. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Apply Now: Junior Collaborator – Institutional Engagement and Development Actors (Remote, IIHL) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Carter Center Educational Programs 2026: Paid Internships and Graduate Assistantships (Complete Guide)
summary: Key Details Organization: The Carter Center Location: Atlanta, Georgia, USA (in-person) Programs: Paid Internships and Graduate Assistantships Focus Areas: Peace, Health, Democracy, Human Rights, International Development, Operations Internship Stipend: Spring (15 weeks): $5,100 Summer (10 weeks): $3,400 Fall (15 weeks): $5,100 Graduate Assistantship Compensation: $18.50 per hour 9 12 months, 20 hours per week Internship Deadlines: Summer 2026: March 2, 2026 (11:59 p.m. ET) Fall 2026: June 15, 2026 (11:59 p.m. ET) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Graduate Assistantship Deadlines: Application Opens: February 13, 2026 Application Deadline: April 23, 2026 (11:59 p.m. ET) Recommendation Letters Deadline: April 30, 2026 ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Who Can Apply: Undergraduate juniors or seniors Graduate students Recent graduates (within 24 months) International students authorized to work in the U.S. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Introduction The Carter Center Educational Programs provide structured professional opportunities for students and early-career professionals interested in advancing global peace, public health, democracy, and operational excellence. With more than four decades of experience and nearly 4,000 participants worldwide, the program has become a recognized pathway for individuals pursuing careers in international development, policy, and nonprofit leadership. Through paid internships and graduate assistantships, participants gain hands-on experience supporting real projects while building practical skills, professional networks, and career direction. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); About the Carter Center Educational Programs The Educational Programs were created to cultivate informed and committed leaders who can contribute meaningfully to global peace and health initiatives. Participants work alongside experts and program teams, applying academic knowledge to practical challenges while gaining exposure to the realities of international development work. The programs emphasize professional growth, mentorship, and experiential learning, helping participants explore career pathways and strengthen technical and leadership competencies. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Carter Center Internship Program Program Overview The Carter Center Internship Program offers diverse opportunities for students and recent graduates to gain professional experience across peace, health, and operational initiatives. Interns contribute directly to ongoing projects while developing skills relevant to careers in international affairs, nonprofit management, public health, and global policy. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet one of the following criteria: Undergraduate students who have completed at least two years of study (minimum 60 credit hours in the U.S. system) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Graduate students currently enrolled or enrolled within the last 24 months Recent graduates who completed their undergraduate degree within the past 24 months Additional requirements include: Authorization to work in the United States No citizenship restrictions International students enrolled at U.S.-based institutions may apply with approval from their host institution ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); All internship opportunities are based in Atlanta, Georgia. Internship Compensation Internships are paid through a stipend structure: Spring Semester (15 weeks): $5,100 Summer Semester (10 weeks): $3,400 Fall Semester (15 weeks): $5,100 Internship Program Areas Internship placements are available across three major divisions: Peace Programs China Focus (opens in new window) Conflict Resolution Program (opens in new window) Democracy (opens in new window) Human Rights Program (opens in new window) Inform Women, Transform Lives (opens in new window) Latin America and Caribbean Focus (opens in new window) Office of the Vice President of Peace (opens in new window) Rule of Law (opens in new window) Health Programs International Public Health (opens in new window) Mental Health Program (opens in new window) Special Health Projects (opens in new window) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Operations Programs Communications (opens in new window) Development (opens in new window) Educational Programs (opens in new window) People Operations Programs (Human Resources) (opens in new window) Information Technology (opens in new window) Strategy, Innovation and Learning (opens in new window) Volunteer Services/Art Collection (opens in new window) Overseas Operations (opens in new window) DEI Internal Communications Intern Experience and Career Impact Participants often describe the internship as a launching point for professional growth. Interns gain exposure to international development leadership, apply academic knowledge in real contexts, and build practical skills valued across the nonprofit and policy sectors. Former interns have contributed to projects related to election observation, governance, and regional initiatives while developing expertise that supports future graduate study and professional advancement. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Apply Today Start your journey at The Carter Center by applying for an internship or graduate assistantship today. Internship Application Carter Center Graduate Assistantship Program Program Overview The Graduate Assistantship Program offers a limited number of funded opportunities for master s and doctoral students seeking deeper engagement in research and program implementation. Graduate assistants work closely with program teams and contribute to substantive projects while balancing academic commitments. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Program Structure Duration: 9 12 months Workload: 20 hours per week Location: Atlanta, Georgia Compensation: $18.50 per hour Eligibility Requirements Applicants must: Be enrolled in a master s or doctoral program throughout the assistantship Have completed at least two semesters of graduate-level coursework at the start of the program Remain enrolled until the end of the assistantship ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Students who graduate before the assistantship concludes are not eligible. Graduate Assistantship Program Areas Peace Conflict Resolution (opens in new window) Democracy Program (opens in new window) Inform Women, Transform Lives (opens in new window) China Focus (opens in new window) Health Mental Health Program (opens in new window) Trachoma Control Program (opens in new window) Operations Development: Donor Landscape Analysis (opens in new window) People Operations Programs Apply Today Start your journey at The Carter Center by applying for an internship or graduate assistantship today. Graduate Assistantship Application Application Dates and Deadlines Internships Summer 2026 Session Application Deadline: March 2, 2026 (11:59 p.m. ET) Start Date: May 19, 2026 Fall 2026 Session Application Deadline: June 15, 2026 (11:59 p.m. ET) Start Date: August 25, 2026 Graduate Assistantships Application Opens: February 13, 2026 Application Deadline: April 23, 2026 (11:59 p.m. ET) Recommendation Letters Deadline: April 30, 2026 Why Apply to the Carter Center Educational Programs These programs are highly valuable for individuals seeking careers in international development and global impact. Key benefits include: Paid professional experience Exposure to peacebuilding and global health initiatives Opportunities to apply academic training in real-world contexts Access to experienced mentors and professionals Strong preparation for graduate study or careers in NGOs, policy, and international organizations ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); How to Apply and Contact Information Applications are submitted through the Carter Center Educational Programs portal. Candidates can apply for internships or graduate assistantships depending on eligibility. Apply Today Start your journey at The Carter Center by applying for an internship or graduate assistantship today. Internship Application Graduate Assistantship Application For questions and eligibility clarification: Email: EducationalPrograms@CarterCenter.org Conclusion The Carter Center Educational Programs offer a competitive and practical pathway for students and early-career professionals committed to advancing peace and health worldwide. With paid positions, structured mentorship, and meaningful project experience, the internships and graduate assistantships provide strong preparation for long-term careers in international development and global leadership. Applicants are encouraged to prepare early and submit strong, well-tailored applications before the deadlines. Source: https://www.cartercenter.org/educational-programs/#dates The post Carter Center Educational Programs 2026: Paid Internships and Graduate Assistantships (Complete Guide) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: NYC Teaching Fellows 2026: Become a Fully Certified NYC Public School Teacher by Fall 2026 (Starting Salary $71,314+)
summary: Are you ready to launch a meaningful career in education while making a direct impact in high-need communities? The NYC Teaching Fellows Program 2026 offers a highly selective, fully supported pathway to become a certified teacher in New York City Public Schools by Fall 2026. Designed for talented professionals and recent graduates from diverse backgrounds, the program prepares participants to teach in critical shortage subject areas such as math, science, special education, and bilingual education while earning a full-time salary and working toward a subsidized master s degree. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); If you are passionate about educational equity, student success, and leadership in urban education, this opportunity could redefine your career trajectory. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About NYC Teaching Fellows NYC Teaching Fellows is a rigorous alternative certification program operated by NYC Public Schools the largest public school system in the United States, serving approximately 1 million students across more than 1,600 schools. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The program recruits individuals committed to addressing educational inequities in underserved communities. Fellows receive intensive training, ongoing coaching, and professional development while working full-time as teachers. By Fall 2026, selected candidates will be placed in NYC Public Schools classrooms and begin their careers as certified educators. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Program Structure and Timeline Summer 2026 Intensive Pre-Service Training Participants begin with a 7-week intensive summer training program designed to build foundational teaching competencies, including: Instructional planning Classroom management Curriculum development Student assessment strategies Equity-centered pedagogy Fellows receive a stipend during this training period. September 2026 Full-Time Teaching Placement In Fall 2026, Fellows: Begin teaching full-time in NYC Public Schools Earn a starting salary of $71,314+ per year Receive full employee benefits Continue working toward initial teaching certification ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Graduate Education Component Participants simultaneously pursue a subsidized master s degree in education at a partner institution. This dual-track model allows Fellows to: Gain hands-on classroom experience Complete graduate coursework Earn certification Develop advanced pedagogical skills Compensation and Benefits The NYC Teaching Fellows program offers a competitive financial and professional package: Starting Salary $71,314+ per year as a full-time NYC Public Schools teacher Summer Training Stipend Participants receive financial support during the 7-week summer training period. Full Benefits Package Medical insurance Dental insurance Vision insurance Pension plan 401(k) Commuter benefits Tuition assistance Student loan assistance ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Professional Development Ongoing mentorship Coaching support Access to a network of dedicated educators Structured professional growth pathway High-Need Subject Areas NYC Teaching Fellows recruits candidates for shortage subject areas, including: Mathematics Science Special Education Bilingual Education Specific subject eligibility requirements may apply. Applicants are encouraged to review subject-specific criteria HERE . Who Should Apply? NYC Teaching Fellows seeks candidates who demonstrate: Commitment to educational equity Resilience and adaptability Growth mindset Passion for student success Willingness to work in high-need communities ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Importantly, no prior teaching experience is required. Eligibility Requirements To apply for the Fall 2026 cohort, candidates must: Hold a bachelor s degree by May 31, 2026 Have a minimum undergraduate GPA of 2.5 Be fluent in written and spoken English Have permanent authorization to work in the United States Never have participated in a program leading to U.S. or Puerto Rico teacher certification Meet additional subject-specific requirements where applicable Why Choose NYC Public Schools? NYC Public Schools is the largest public school system in the U.S., employing: 75,000+ teachers Thousands of counselors, social workers, administrators, and staff The system reflects New York City s vibrant diversity and is deeply committed to equity, inclusion, and excellence. By joining NYC Teaching Fellows, participants become part of a workforce dedicated to ensuring every student is seen, supported, and set up to thrive. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Equal Opportunity Employer The NYC Department of Education is committed to nondiscrimination and equal opportunity in employment and education. Opportunities are provided without regard to race, color, religion, ethnicity, national origin, citizenship status, age, marital status, disability, sexual orientation, gender, military status, or other protected characteristics. How to Apply Find the application link HERE . Click HERE to find the program information. Interested candidates are encouraged to apply early due to the competitive and highly selective nature of the program. Discover more job opportunities on OFY HERE .
